channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Allstate Sugar Bowl postponed
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)—The Caesars Superdome was placed under lockdown Wednesday after a pickup truck drove into a New Year’s crowd about a mile away, killing at least 15 people and injuring dozens, city and federal officials said.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Top-seeded Oregon sees title hopes dashed with early knockout by Ohio State
item.description: PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Oregon withstood many challenges en route to being the only unbeaten team and the top seed in the College Football Playoff.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pelicans players, coach grieve those killed in New Orleans attack as they play in Miami
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Trey Murphy III knew his mother was in downtown New Orleans to ring in the new year. His chef was there as well. And when Murphy heard about the tragedy that happened on Bourbon Street, he was scared for their safety. The word eventually came: They were fine.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Penn State takes simple approach during ride into CFP semifinals
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Football coaches, perhaps more than in other sports, are huge proponents of structure, routine, consistent messaging. Since the early days of his time in Happy Valley, Penn State's James Franklin has adhered to the philosophy of 1-0. Yes, it's a cliche.
item.dc:creator: JOHN MARSHALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eagles coach Nick Sirianni set to rest Saquon Barkley in finale and end chance at NFL rushing record
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Saquon Barkley had a heart-to-heart talk with his dad that was more coated in heartbreak than hope over the Philadelphia Eagles running back's pursuit of the NFL single-season rushing record.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Targeting no-call at Peach Bowl raises more questions about disputed rule
item.description: The Peach Bowl referee declined to call a targeting penalty late in the fourth quarter for a helmet-to-helmet hit by Texas safety Michael Taaffe that could’ve given Arizona State a chance to kick a winning field goal in the College Football Playoff quarterfinals.
item.dc:creator: Paul Newberry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Skattebo's do-it-all show is not enough to overcome Arizona State's 4th-down woes against Texas
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Cam Skattebo's do-it-all offensive show wasn't enough to save Arizona State from a stretch of fourth-down failures against Texas. Led by Skattebo, the senior running back, Arizona State compiled big advantages in rushing yards, total yards and time of possession.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Saquon Barkley and family react to Eagles sitting him with chance to break NFL rushing record
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles player Saquon Barkley addressed his team's decision to rest him in the last game of the season, skipping a chance to break a league record.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saquon Barkley won't play vs. Giants, sacrificing chance to break NFL rushing record
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles running back Saquon Barkley is set to sit out Sunday's game against the New York Giants, despite a chance to break the NFL's rushing record.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SEC commissioner speaks out on Sugar Bowl postponement amid New Orleans attack as fans, pundits weigh in
item.description: Football fans and pundits on social media were divided about the decision to postpone the Sugar Bowl in the wake of Wednesday's New Orleans attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Garoppolo to make debut for playoff-bound Rams in place of Matthew Stafford against Seahawks
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Jimmy Garoppolo will take his first snaps for the Rams when he starts in place of Matthew Stafford in playoff-bound Los Angeles' regular-season finale against Seattle on Sunday. Coach Sean McVay announced the decision Wednesday to rest Stafford, the 16-year veteran starter.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Chiefs to start Carson Wentz at QB against Denver, which would clinch a playoff berth with a win
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — The Chiefs will start backup Carson Wentz at quarterback on Sunday in Denver in a game that means nothing to Kansas City, which has already clinched the No. 1 seed in the AFC playoffs, but could mean everything to the Broncos.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sugar Bowl CFP quarterfinal between Georgia and Notre Dame postponed after deadly truck attack
item.description: The College Football Playoff quarterfinal in New Orleans has been postponed a day after a truck plowed into a New Year’s crowd about a mile away from the site of the Sugar Bowl between Georgia and Notre Dame.
item.dc:creator: Brett Martel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Notre Dame calls on fans to 'join us in prayer’ following apparent terror attack ahead of Sugar Bowl
item.description: The University of Notre Dame is calling on fans in New Orleans ahead of the Sugar Bowl to join in prayer following a "senseless act of violence" that left at least 10 people dead and injured more than two dozen others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jeanty falls short of record as dream season gets stuffed by Penn State defense in CFP loss
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Ashton Jeanty took the handoff on Boise State's first play and made it just a few steps before being greeted by a wall of dark blue Penn State jerseys that swallowed him up and shoved him backward for no gain.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Women's rights activists call for England to forfeit championship cricket match to Afghanistan
item.description: Women's rights activists have called for England's cricket team to forfeit its championship match against Afghanistan in February.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lenny Randle, major leaguer repeatedly in the spotlight for 12 seasons, dies at 75
item.description: MURRIETA, Calif. (AP) — Lenny Randle, a big league player for 12 seasons who spoke five languages, performed stand-up comedy, was dubbed “The Most Interesting Man in Baseball” and was suspended for punching his Texas Rangers manager, has died. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Quinn Ewers' former Texas teammate posts cryptic message after rumor of $6 million offer for QB to transfer
item.description: Quinn Ewers reportedly has a $6 million offer to transfer out of Texas instead of going pro, but his former teammate said that would be a waste of money.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PHOTOS: Blues, Blackhawks close 2024 with the Winter Classic
item.description: It's Winter Classic Day in the Windy City on New Year's Eve. The ultimate outdoor hockey showdown between the cross-state rival St. Louis Blues and Chicago Blackhawks is underway.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Holiday Classic: Lincoln Stockton 64, Mission Bay 45
item.description: In the American Bracket Championship Game of the 2024 Holiday Classic, Lincoln High School from Stockton, CA defeated Mission Bay 64-45.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Coby White, Torrey Craig lead the Bulls past the Hornets in overtime, 115-108
item.description: Coby White narrowly missed his first career triple-double, finishing with 23 points, 10 rebounds and nine assists, and Torrey Craig scored 18 with five 3-pointers after halftime as the Chicago Bulls held on to beat the Charlotte Hornets 115-108 in overtime Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2024 Holiday Bowl: Homegrown Cougars leave it all on the field in front of hometown crowd
item.description: While a depleted Washington State may not have tasted any eggnog this year in the Holiday Bowl after falling short to Syracuse 52-35, a few San Diego locals still making a memory of a lifetime playing on the big stage in front of their hometown crowd.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Jake Evans scores for the career-high 4th consecutive game, surging Canadiens beat Lightning 5-2
item.description: Jake Evans scored for the career-high fifth consecutive game and the surging Montreal Canadiens beat the Tampa Bay Lightning 5-2 on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: ERIK ERLENDSSON Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayfield throws 5 TD passes and Bucs keep playoff, NFC South hopes alive with 48-14 rout of Panthers
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — The Tampa Bay Buccaneers played like a team determined to do whatever's necessary to make the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: FRED GOODALL, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Clearwater Beach hosts ReliaQuest Bowl Beach Day
item.description: The ReliaQuest Bowl Beach Day on Clearwater Beach kicked off at 11 a.m. with the festivities. But fans started lining their beach chairs up for a close view of the action much earlier.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Brew, WFLA, NBC

item.title: BYU crushes Colorado in Alamo Bowl, 36-14
item.description: SAN ANTONIO, Tex. (AP) - L.J. Martin rushed for 93 yards and two touchdowns, Jake Retzlaff passed for 151 yards, and No. 17 BYU beat 20th-ranked Colorado 36-14 in the Alamo Bowl on Saturday night. “Understanding that they have great players on the other side of the field," Martin said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: USU beats San Diego State on last-second shot, 67-66
item.description: SAN DIEGO, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah State men’s basketball earned its first road win over a ranked opponent in over three decades Saturday afternoon in front of a sellout crowd at Viejas Arena, taking down No. 20 San Diego State 67-66.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Dodgers' Ohtani and wife will soon be welcoming a baby to the family
item.description: Between a marriage announcement, third MVP award and World Series title, Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani had a pretty good 2024. And now, it looks like 2025 may be even better for the Japanese two-way baseball phenom.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel dies from cancer at age 78
item.description: Greg Gumbel, a longtime CBS sportscaster who broke barriers during his career calling some of the biggest sporting events, has died from cancer, according to a statement from his family released by the network on Friday. He was 78.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cathedral Catholic goalie shows why she's a keeper; honors her coach through art
item.description: Cathedral Catholic's 4-year starting goalie Maria'O Brien expresses her love to her head soccer coach through art. Suprising and touching longtime Cathedral Coach Dawn Lee.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Holiday Bowl to kick off at brand-new Snapdragon Stadium for the first time
item.description: The Holiday Bowl has been woven into the fabric of San Diego for four decades. For a football city without a professional sports team, there is even more excitement surrounding this annual event.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: DIRECTV Holiday Bowl: What to know before going
item.description: It will be the first time the Holiday Bowl is held in Mission Valley — where it had been for more than 40 years — since before the pandemic. The two most recent bowl games were played in Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
